summary: `.github/workflows/codex.yml` adds a new `/autoresolve` issue-comment trigger for issue auto-resolution, but no matching doc/spec text was updated and the job header now describes only “new issues.” In this repo docs are the spec, so the new trigger is currently undocumented/stale.

blocking_issues.message: `.github/workflows/codex.yml:153-159` and `:382-384` add a new `/autoresolve` issue-comment path into `resolve-issue`, but the only nearby description at `:372` still says the job auto-resolves "new issues" and no spec/contributor doc was updated to define the new command. In a docs-as-spec repo, introducing a new human-facing trigger only in workflow code leaves runtime behavior undocumented and the existing description stale.

fix_suggestions.patch_hint: Update the owning documentation for `Codex Agent` triggers to describe both auto-resolution entry points: issue lifecycle events (`opened`/`reopened`/`unlabeled` after `codex-started`) and the new `/autoresolve` issue-comment command. Also refresh the stale job header comment in `.github/workflows/codex.yml` so it no longer says only "new issues".
